Nav noise with many links ABCDEF

The Rust Programming Language, 2nd Edition

by Steve Klabnik and Carol Nichols
$29.99

The Rust Programming Language is the official book on Rust, written by the Rust development team at Mozilla. It covers the language comprehensively, from installation and basic syntax to advanced topics like ownership, lifetimes, closures, iterators, and concurrency.

In the book, you will learn:

  • How Rust's ownership system prevents memory-safety bugs at compile time
  • When and how to use structs, enums, and pattern matching
  • How to write tests and document your code
  • How to build command-line tools and web servers

This book assumes no prior knowledge of Rust, but familiarity with another programming language is helpful. The text is available free online at doc.rust-lang.org/book.

Customer Reviews

5 stars — Excellent introduction to Rust
4 stars — Dense but rewarding
